programs.name: Med-QUEST (Medicaid for seniors/disabled)
programs.what_it_does: Med-QUEST administers Medicaid in Hawaii, providing health coverage including long-term care services for eligible seniors and disabled individuals. It serves as the primary safety net for medical needs not covered by Medicare.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+, legally blind individuals, or those totally and permanently disabled with low income and limited resources.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers medical services, long-term care, and cash assistance via AABD for essentials like food and shelter.

programs.name: Kūpuna Care Program
programs.what_it_does: The Kūpuna Care Program delivers community-based long-term care services to enable seniors to remain at home. It funds home and community-based services waiver programs through contracted agencies.
programs.who_its_for: Hawaii residents aged 60+ with functional impairments, U.S. citizens or qualified aliens, not residing in institutions or covered by comparable services.
programs.benefit_value: Community-based services including case management, personal care, and home support; value depends on assessed needs.

programs.name: Sage PLUS Program (PACE equivalent)
programs.what_it_does: Sage PLUS provides comprehensive health and long-term care coordination for frail seniors, integrating medical and social services. It functions similarly to federal PACE by supporting aging in place through day health and home services.
programs.who_its_for: Medicare-eligible seniors aged 55+ who are nursing facility-eligible and live in service areas.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitated care covering all Medicare and Medicaid services; monthly premium or copay based on income.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help pay Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, and copayments for low-income seniors. Administered via Med-QUEST as Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), and Qualifying Individual (QI).
programs.who_its_for: Medicare enrollees with incomes below specific federal poverty levels (e.g., 100-135% FPL depending on program) and limited resources.
programs.benefit_value: Full/partial coverage of premiums (up to $185/month for Part B), deductibles ($240/year Part B), and cost-sharing.

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing nutritious food at approved retailers. It supplements food budgets for low-income households including seniors.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+, disabled, or aged/blind individuals meeting income (e.g., <130% FPL) and resource limits.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per eligible senior household, depending on income and household size.

programs.name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP offers grants to help pay home energy bills and address heating/cooling needs. It prevents utility shutoffs and improves energy efficiency for vulnerable households.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households (≤150% FPL) including seniors, disabled, or those with high energy burdens.
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants averaging $300-700 per household for bill payment or crisis assistance.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides home energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and efficient appliances. It reduces energy costs and improves health/safety for low-income homes.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income (≤200% FPL) households with seniors, disabled, or children; priority for elderly.
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements valued at $3,000-8,000 per household.

programs.name: Hawaii SHIP (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free, personalized Medicare counseling, education, and assistance with enrollment. Counselors help with Medicare options, appeals, and low-income aids.
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries, caregivers, and pre-retirees seeking insurance guidance.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; saves thousands via proper enrollment and aid access.

programs.name: Home-Delivered Meals (via Kūpuna Aging in Place grantees)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Provided through nonprofits funded for kūpuna services.
programs.who_its_for: Low-to-moderate income seniors 65+ who are homebound and unable to prepare meals.
programs.benefit_value: 5-7 meals/week; annual value ~$2,000-3,000 per participant.

programs.name: Caregiver Support (via Executive Office on Aging)
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite care, training, counseling, and support groups for family caregivers. Helps sustain caregiving to support kūpuna aging in place.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors 60+ or adults 18+ with disabilities like Alzheimer's.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $500-1,000/year; training and groups at no cost.

programs.name: Retired Senior Volunteer Program (RSVP, related to SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Connects seniors 55+ to paid part-time community service jobs with stipends. Builds skills, income, and community ties while addressing workforce needs.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income adults 55+ seeking work experience and wages.
programs.benefit_value: Stipends ~$3-4/hour for 20 hours/week; annual earnings up to $7,000-10,000.

programs.name: Senior Legal Helpline (via Area Agencies on Aging)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal advice, representation, and education on elder rights issues. Covers benefits, housing, abuse, and consumer problems.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing legal barriers to services or rights violations.
programs.benefit_value: Free consultations and case help; saves $1,000s in legal fees.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program (LTCOP)
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, resolving complaints and protecting rights. Offers confidential assistance and systemic advocacy.
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities; families/caregivers.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy services; improves care quality and resolves 80%+ of complaints.

programs.name: Kahi Malama – A Place of Caring
programs.what_it_does: Serves as the Aging & Disability Resource Center for Hawai’i County, providing planning, coordination, advocacy, and administration of programs for older persons. Offers access to home and community-based services including case management, personal care, and protective services for vulnerable adults regardless of income.
programs.who_its_for: Older adults aged 60+ and individuals with disabilities on the Big Island (Hawai’i County), including frail elders and vulnerable adults.
programs.whats_unique: County-specific Area Agency on Aging (AAA) with branches in Hilo and Kona, delivering localized protective and community services not replicated identically in other states' structures.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by service; free information/referral, income-based eligibility for home care and protective services.

programs.name: Elderly Affairs Division (EAD) / Honolulu ADRC
programs.what_it_does: Acts as the Area Agency on Aging for Oʻahu, contracting with organizations for services like case management, personal care, homemaker care, home-delivered meals, and housing assistance. Operates a helpline for consultation, information, and referral while providing outreach and education.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 60+ and caregivers in Honolulu County (Oʻahu), including frail elders needing in-home support.
programs.whats_unique: Sponsors city-specific ADRC programs tailored to Oʻahu's urban kupuna needs, with contracted services emphasizing community partnerships unique to Hawaii's island demographics.
programs.benefit_value: Contracted services at low/no cost based on income; helpline free.

programs.name: Kaua’i Agency on Elderly Affairs
programs.what_it_does: Provides aging services coordination, advocacy, and direct support through the Kauai ADRC, including access to home and community-based services. Facilitates programs for independent living via referrals to local resources.
programs.who_its_for: Older adults 60+ and disabled individuals on Kauaʻi island.
programs.whats_unique: Island-specific agency addressing rural Kauai's unique geographic challenges, distinct from mainland state models.
programs.benefit_value: Free referrals and coordination; service costs vary by eligibility.

programs.name: Home and Community Services (Statewide via EOA)
programs.what_it_does: Offers services like adult day care, assisted transportation, attendant care, case management, chore services, congregate/home-delivered meals, personal care, and nutrition education to promote independence. Delivered through county offices on aging across all islands.
programs.who_its_for: Older adults aged 60+ who are frail or have disabilities, needing support to remain at home.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded array coordinated by Executive Office on Aging (EOA), customized for Hawaii's multi-island, culturally diverse kupuna population beyond standard federal programs.
programs.benefit_value: Low-cost or free based on income; e.g., door-to-door transport, meals (specific $ values not listed).

programs.name: Transportation Friends for Kupuna (TFK)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free door-through-door transportation and escort assistance via volunteers. Helps independent seniors access essential destinations when they cannot drive.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 60+ living independently on Oahu who need transport support.
programs.whats_unique: Volunteer-driven, Hawaii-specific program by Catholic Charities focused on Oahu's kupuna, emphasizing aloha culture in peer assistance.
programs.benefit_value: Free transportation and escort services.

programs.name: Senior Companion Program
programs.what_it_does: Matches income-eligible seniors 55+ as companions to serve frail seniors with oversight and social support. Promotes community engagement while aiding isolated elders.
programs.who_its_for: Income-eligible persons 55+ volunteering to assist frail seniors; recipients are vulnerable older adults.
programs.whats_unique: State-administered via Adult & Community Care Branch, tailored for Hawaii's aging population with cultural sensitivity.
programs.benefit_value: Stipend for companions; free service to frail seniors.

programs.name: Foster Grandparent Program
programs.what_it_does: Recruits income-eligible persons 55+ to serve children with special needs in schools, fostering intergenerational support. Provides structured volunteer roles with training.
programs.who_its_for: Income-eligible adults 55+ interested in mentoring children; indirectly benefits senior mental health.
programs.whats_unique: Hawaii DHS program uniquely linking kupuna to keiki (children) in school settings, reflecting island community values.
programs.benefit_value: Stipend and benefits for participants 55+.

programs.name: Memory Mentor Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers free in-home visits by trained mentors for seniors with memory loss living alone. Provides companionship, safety checks, and light support to delay institutionalization.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors with memory loss (e.g., dementia) living alone with minimal caregiving on Oahu.
programs.whats_unique: Catholic Charities Hawaii program specifically for isolated kupuna with cognitive decline, volunteer-based and home-focused.
programs.benefit_value: Free in-home service.
